name: Bahamian Peas and Rice
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Salt and freshly ground pepper
3/4 cup Cooked pigeon peas (black-eyed peas can be substituted)
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry the salt pork in a large skillet until the fat has been released. Add the vegetable oil.
2. Add the onion and sauté until translucent.
3. Add the green pepper, celery, thyme, and tomato paste. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Add the peas and cook for another 5 minutes.
5. Transfer the pea mixture to a saucepan. Add water and salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a boil.
6. Add the rice and stir. Reduce heat when the water returns to a boil. Cook, uncovered, for about 25 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed.
7. This dish is usually served as a side dish.
